answers.q1_scan_12m_sources: I'd love that. Right now I manually search Google Images and TinEye for my character designs. Some fan translations repost my panels on aggregator sites within hours. A 12M source scan would catch those Chinese and Korean piracy sites I can't even search.
answers.q2_auto_takedown: Auto-takedown would save me hours. I currently file DMCA manually on Twitter, Instagram, and three manga piracy sites per week. If it could auto-file on platforms that support it, I'd pay double.
answers.q3_invisible_watermark: Critical for comic panels. People crop, edit, and repost constantly. If I could embed invisible watermarks that survive cropping and filter application, I could prove ownership when art theft disputes arise.
answers.q4_blockchain_timestamp: Essential. I draw chapters serially over weeks. Timestamping each panel as I finish would establish my创作 timeline against copycats who claim they drew it first.
answers.q5_c2pa_webtoon: I publish on Webtoon and Tapas. C2PA certification per chapter would give readers and platforms verifiable proof of authenticity. Especially useful when piracy sites steal entire series.
answers.q6_missing_from_protection: Character design sheets and reference materials. Most protection focuses on finished art, but my OC reference sheets get stolen constantly and used by other artists.
answers.q7_one_feature_to_add: Batch upload with series tracking. I need to upload 20-30 panels per chapter and have them all linked as one series for monitoring.
answers.q8_daily_usage_trigger: Every time I post a new panel or chapter. I'd check right after uploading to my publishing platforms.
answers.q9_unhandled_needs: Monitoring for trace and redraw theft. Someone takes my panel, traces over it, and posts it as original. Current tools don't catch that.
answers.q10_how_often_check: Daily. Art theft in manga is rampant and fast. Within 24 hours of posting, pirated copies are already circulating.
answers.q11_platforms: Twitter/X, Pixiv, Webtoon, Tapas, Instagram, and three Chinese manga sites I've found through reverse searches.
answers.q12_pieces_per_month: 15-20 finished panels plus sketches and character sheets. So roughly 40-50 individual pieces.
answers.q13_current_workflow: Post to Twitter first, then Pixiv and Webtoon. Do a manual Google reverse image search weekly. File DMCA when I find theft. It's exhausting.
answers.q14_upload_today_barriers: Would upload immediately. No barriers if it's free and handles batch uploads. My only concern is file size limits for high-res manga pages.
answers.q15_trust: Cautious. I'd want to know where my art data is stored and who has access. AI companies scraping art is a huge concern in our community.
answers.q16_ai_analyzing: Comfortable if it's only analyzing for theft detection and not training on my art. Need a clear no-training guarantee.
answers.q17_recommend: Absolutely. Every manga artist I know deals with piracy. This would be instant word-of-mouth in our community.
answers.q18_must_do: Series-level monitoring that links chapters together. Individual piece monitoring misses the big picture of full series piracy.
